front: What is HPLC and how does it separate peptides?
back: High-Performance Liquid Chromatography separates peptides based on hydrophobicity (reverse-phase), charge (ion-exchange), or size (SEC). RP-HPLC (C18 column) is most common. Elution uses acetonitrile/water gradient with TFA. Detection: UV at 214/220 nm (peptide bond absorption).
